journal_entry.rationale: Inventory loss from natural disaster is recognized as an expense (loss). The asset account Inventory is reduced (credited) and a Loss on Inventory Destruction expense account is debited. Under IFRS, losses from natural disasters are recognized in profit or loss in the period they occur. The tuples correctly identify this as expense increase (debit) and asset decrease (credit). No tax implications as this is a loss event, not a taxable supply or purchase.

output.reason: Inventory destroyed = asset decrease. The inventory account must be credited to remove the destroyed goods from the books. The loss will be debited to an expense or loss account.

credit_classifier.reason: Inventory destroyed = asset decrease. The inventory account must be credited to remove the destroyed goods from the books. The loss will be debited to an expense or loss account.

output.reason: Inventory destroyed by typhoon is an abnormal loss/casualty. The debit side records the loss as an expense increase (casualty loss or inventory loss expense). The inventory asset itself will be credited (decreased) on the credit side.
